Episode Summary The Troubleshooters yo to their briefing, room and receive mission instructions. h? Pardon me? You say it sounds simple? You must be new around here 
Getting There ‘Te Troubleshooters have ther insiructions Repost to Briefing Room IOGC. They even have a map. What could be easier? Well, here's the problem. The map is about the size ofan average fingernail — way too tiny to ead, Obviously, the Troubleshooters rust enlarge the map So they can read it, or divine the location of Briefing Room |OGC in some other way. Here are a couple of things they could try: Get a Magnifying Glass: These are available through the black market (at 30 credits each) or they can be requisitioned at PLC Outing and Supply. To get one through PLC, the Troubleshooter simply fils out an equipment request form, hands it in to the Yellow PLC Clerk, and wait for the inevitable “I'm sorry ‘That isn’t available at your security clearance. [At your service” which will be delivered with ‘a supercilious smirk accompanied by an open palm (a universally-understood gesture mean- ing roughly, “Gimme’) Te they accuse the clerk of treason — well, he's Yellow clearance; they're Red. Who do you think everybody's going to believe? Right. WF they attempt to slip him less than 25 credits, the clerk thanks them effusively. He tells them that he will get right on it f the 
PC #4 Js told by R&D that HPD & MC ans no report of this mission to return to the Armed Forces. It’s possible that the HPD members of the party have orders to Kall everybody to prevent this. 
PC #5 is told by CPU that this mission is being set upto embarrass CPU and that he 1s likely to be set up as the pigeon’ scapegoat if anything goes wrong. Watch your back, and avoid taking any responsibility P46 is told by HPD & MC to keep a close ‘watch for improper form filing procedures by other Troubleshooters. CPU is bearing dovin hard on HPD; HPD is trying io keep its nose. clean until things settle down,

sgentleclones will be so kind as to wait just a moment. The clerk strides purposefully through a door marked “Office” and goes off to lunch, He returns three hours later and claims to never have heard of the PCs. Ifthe PCs cough up 25 credits or more, the clerk issues them a temporary equipme ‘usage permit and lets them look through microscope Enlarged, the map is revealed as mean ingless scribble. 
‘Ask The Computer for the Location of the Briefing Room: “At your service...What is your security clearancee...'m sorry, that infor- mation is not available at your security clearance...Perhaps you would care to explain why your request for classified information isn't part of an insidious Commie plot, Citizen?” ‘At that point, one of two things happens: 1, one or more of the other PCs screams “treason” at the top of his voice and the episode degenerates into a messy free-for-all; (oF 2, the other Troubleshooters permit the citizen to explain to The Computer why he isn’t a traitor. lithe PCs choose the free-for-all routine, et the combat go on for as long.as iti interesting, and then send in a couple of dozen IntSec Troopers. They capture the survivors, subject them to fairly rigorous interrogation and brai softening, and eventually dump the remains in the hallway outside the !OGC briefing 
Ii the PCs allow the Troubleshooter to speak, the player must make an easy bootlicking or spurious logic skill roll (or you can roleplay it out) to convince Our Pal that he isn’t a Com- mie traitor. Ifhe's successful, The Computer

Episode One: The Briefing 
ives him the necessary instructions; fhe fails, ‘The Computer assigns him a treason point or two and the entire team is arrested, inter- rogated, brain-softened, and dumped, as above. 
Request the Briefing Room's Location From Secret Society or Service Group Contacts: This can either be noted as a simple expen: diture of an IU, or played out as an exciting, thvilLa-minute roleplaying extravaganza where sectet passwords, bribes, and tense intimida- tion skill rolls lead to clandestine meetings with shady NPCs in dark, smelly, unused- looking hallways. Whichever you choose, the PC should eventually learn the way to the briefing room, Of course, explaining to his ever-vigilanty- looking-for-Communiststo-exterminate fellow Troubleshooters exactly how he learned this Classified information could be a tad dicey. 
GMing Note: See what’s going on here? The PCs! need to find out where their briefing room is. No one will tell them. Why not? Because Understand? This is referred to in the vernacular as a “hose job." It is a common feature of well- designed Paranoia adventures, and one that the players will eventually develop a healthy skepticism about.
